entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: Words that have not themselves become objects

entities.name: grahaṇa
entities.cite: the word must first itself be grasped

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: signification is routed, ineliminably, through cognition

axioms.name: purvatara
axioms.cite: the priority of grasping-the-word over grasping-the-meaning is the sphota doctrine's account of what hearing is

axioms.name: virayate
axioms.cite: For meaning to be conveyed, the word must first itself be grasped — visayatvam apannaih, having become an object of cognition

denials.name: prakasaka
denials.cite: A word existing unperceived in the vicinity conveys nothing

denials.name: satta_prakasaka
denials.cite: Any theory on which words signify by their sheer existence — by an unmediated relation between real sound and real object that bypasses the hearer's apprehension — is ruled out

rejected_readings.label: direct_acoustic_signification
rejected_readings.rendering: Words signify meanings by their acoustic properties alone, regardless of whether the hearer perceives them.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the core denial: signification requires grahana; words existing unperceived convey nothing. This reading treats signification as a property of the sound-object relation independent of cognition.

rejected_readings.label: presence_without_apprehension
rejected_readings.rendering: Words present in proximity to the hearer convey their meanings even without being heard or grasped.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the denial that unperceived words convey nothing. This reverses the priority of grasping-the-word, making signification independent of the hearer's cognition.

notes: The verse encodes the epistemological priority of apprehension (grahana purvatara artha) and the dependence of signification on cognition (grahana asraya prakasaka). The formalization captures the sphota doctrine's two-stage hearing process: word-grasping must precede meaning-illumination. Denials explicitly rule out both direct acoustic signification and unmediated presence-based conveyance.
